doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Uh, hi, Dr. Lee. So, um, I hurt my right ankle playing rugby yesterday. I fell on the side of my foot, and now it really hurts, like, a lot. It's swollen and bruised, and I can’t really walk on it.

doctor_turn: When did the pain start, and has it changed since then?
patient_turn: The pain started right after the injury, um, like, yesterday. It’s been pretty sharp, you know, around a 7 or 8 out of 10. I don’t think it’s gotten better at all, honestly. It still hurts a lot, especially when I try to move it.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the pain in your ankle? Does it spread to any other areas?
patient_turn: It's mostly, um, localized to the lateral malleolus, like, the outside part of my ankle. I don’t really feel pain on the inside or the top of my foot, just that side. It’s pretty intense there, though.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s definitely sharp, um, like, really intense when I touch it or try to move my ankle. I guess it’s kind of a stabbing pain, you know? It’s hard to ignore, and it makes me kinda worried.

doctor_turn: What were you doing when the pain started?
patient_turn: Oh, I was just sitting on the couch when it started, not doing anything active at all, really. I definitely didn’t hurt it while playing rugby or anything like that.

doctor_turn: How has the pain changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Um, it hasn’t really changed much since it started. It’s still really sharp and painful, and I can’t put any weight on it. I was hoping it would get better, but it just feels the same, honestly.

doctor_turn: Are you having difficulty moving your ankle up or down?
patient_turn: Yeah, definitely. I’m having a lot of trouble with both dorsiflexion and plantar flexion. It really hurts when I try to move it, so I just, um, try not to move it at all. It’s frustrating, you know?

doctor_turn: Are you having any trouble walking or limping on that ankle?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’m having a lot of trouble walking. I can’t really put any weight on it without it hurting a lot, so I’m limping and favoring my left foot. It’s, um, pretty tough to get around right now.

doctor_turn: Have you ever injured your ankle before this incident?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any previous ankle injuries, um, at least not that I can remember. This is the first time I’ve hurt it like this, and it’s really, uh, concerning for me.

doctor_turn: Are you feeling any numbness or tingling in your foot or ankle?
patient_turn: No, I’m not feeling any numbness or tingling, um, just pain in the ankle itself. It’s mostly just sharp pain, and I don’t have any other weird sensations, thankfully.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any fever or chills since the injury?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any fever or chills or anything like that. It’s just the pain in my ankle, um, and I’m not feeling sick otherwise. Just, you know, worried about the injury.

doctor_turn: Do you feel any weakness in your legs or feet?
patient_turn: No, I don’t feel any weakness in my legs or feet, um, just the pain in my ankle. I can still move my legs fine, but I can’t really use my right foot at all because of the pain.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any redness or warmth around your ankle?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’ve noticed that it’s a bit warm and there’s some bruising around the ankle, like, about 4 cm. It’s definitely swollen too, which is, um, kind of concerning for me.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any stomach or urinary issues since your injury?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any stomach or urinary issues at all. It’s just the pain in my ankle, um, and nothing else really going on. I’m just focused on that right now.

doctor_turn: Do you have any past medical or surgical history that I should know about?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any significant past medical or surgical history. I’ve been pretty healthy overall, um, and I’m not on any regular medications or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications, even occasionally?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any medications, um, not even occasionally. I don’t have any allergies either, so it’s just, you know, this ankle issue that’s bothering me right now.

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications or other substances?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any known allergies to medications or anything else. I’ve never had a reaction to anything, um, so that’s not a concern for me.

doctor_turn: Are your immunizations up to date?
patient_turn: Yeah, my immunizations are up to date. I’ve kept up with them, um, so that’s not something I’m worried about. It’s just this ankle injury that’s got me feeling anxious.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with my parents and siblings. It’s, um, a pretty busy household, but they’re supportive. I guess they’re worried about me too, especially with this injury.

doctor_turn: Do you smoke, drink alcohol, or use any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t smoke or drink alcohol, and I don’t use any recreational drugs. I’m, um, pretty straight-edge about that stuff. Just trying to focus on my health and, you know, recover from this injury.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of health issues, like musculoskeletal or autoimmune diseases?
patient_turn: No, there’s no family history of musculoskeletal or autoimmune diseases that I know of. Um, my family has been pretty healthy overall, so that’s not something I’m concerned about.
